stratum: Algebra II: multivariate & multilinear (the modern representation)
prerequisites: MLE (multilinear extension) — the object summed over the Boolean hypercube
polynomials in several variables and their (per-variable) degree
summation/expectation over a finite domain — the sum over {0,1}^n
the Schwartz–Zippel lemma — the per-round soundness source (carried from Ch 7)
proof by mathematical induction — the soundness argument is an induction on rounds
the notion of a Boolean / arithmetic circuit and its add/mult gates
modular arithmetic in F_p / finite fields (carried from Ch 7)
probability / the union bound (carried from Ch 7)
assumed_background: From Ch 7: the multilinear extension (MLE), the Schwartz–Zippel lemma (proved), low-degree extension, finite fields — load-bearing in full.
From Ch 6: arithmetized constraints and the Sudoku encoding (the constraints that get summed); the 'spot-check' intuition this chapter completes into a protocol.
From Ch 1–2: random challenges, bounded error, and soundness as a felt promise ('almost always'); the verifier asks random questions a cheater cannot anticipate.
Comfort manipulating the Ch 7 objects: evaluating a multivariate polynomial at a point, reading off a univariate's degree.
Mathematical induction stated sharply enough to chain a per-round bound into an overall bound.
learning_objectives: State the sum-check claim: H = Σ_{x∈{0,1}^n} g(x), and explain why a verifier cannot afford to add the 2^n terms directly.

Describe one round: the prover sends a univariate g_i(X) (the partial sum fixing earlier challenges and summing later variables over {0,1}); the verifier checks g_i(0)+g_i(1) equals the running claim, then picks a challenge r_i and recurses on the (n−1)-variable claim g_i(r_i).
Derive the soundness bound d·v/|F| (per-variable degree d times number of variables v over field size |F|) from Schwartz–Zippel applied once per round, via induction on the rounds.
Compute one full transcript on a concrete polynomial over GF(97): verify g_1(0)+g_1(1)=H and each g_i(0)+g_i(1)=g_{i−1}(r_{i−1}), ending at the single evaluation g(r_1,…,r_n).

Explain why sum-check needs NO commitment scheme — it is a pure interactive reduction assuming oracle access to g — and why that purity makes GKR the first no-commitment proof system the reader meets.

Connect sum-check to its book role: the single most-reused gadget downstream — grand-product/ZeroTest (Ch 9), Spartan and the folding hinge (Ch 10), folding schemes defined on sum-check (Ch 14), LogUp-GKR / zkVM lookups (Ch 15).

graphify_subgraph.interpretation: The live graph places Sum-Check in community 8 with degree 57 — one of the highest-degree god-nodes — and shows it *defining* 'The Sumcheck Protocol: The Hidden Foundation' while being *referenced* by Layer 5 (the sealed certificate), Encoding the Performance, zkVMs, and the secret-performance chapter. The two shortest paths confirm the chapter framing exactly: sum-check *assumes* the MLE (the object summed) and *references* Schwartz–Zippel through Layer-4 arithmetization (the per-round soundness source). The dense conceptually_related_to fan-out to CCS/Spartan/Lasso/HyperNova/LatticeFold/folding schemes corroborates the bible's 'single most-reused gadget downstream' claim. The brief's prerequisites (MLE, Schwartz–Zippel) and forward-role objectives are taken directly from these edges.
